signals.title: Disney Agrees to $10M Civil Penalty and Injunction for Alleged Violations of Children’s Privacy Laws
signals.summary: The DOJ announced a settlement with Disney Worldwide Services Inc. and Disney Entertainment Operations LLC, resolving allegations that Disney violated the Children’s Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA). Disney agreed to pay a $10 million civil penalty and implement measures to ensure compliance with COPPA, particularly concerning its YouTube video content.
key_provisions: Settlement with Disney for COPPA violations
Disney to pay $10 million civil penalty
Implementation of compliance measures for COPPA

signals.title: Eastern District of Virginia Prosecutes 19 Child Exploitation Cases Under Project Safe Childhood
signals.summary: The U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District of Virginia reported prosecuting 19 child exploitation cases in March 2026 under the Project Safe Childhood initiative. This included seven new cases, seven convictions, and eight substantial sentences, highlighting the DOJ's ongoing efforts to combat child sexual exploitation.
key_provisions: Seven new cases, seven convictions, eight sentences

signals.title: Long Island Child Therapist Sentenced to 84 Months for Distributing Child Pornography
signals.summary: A licensed social worker was sentenced to 84 months in federal prison for distributing child pornography, underscoring the DOJ's commitment to prosecuting individuals who exploit children.
key_provisions: Sentencing to 84 months in federal prison

signals.title: Revisions to the Children's Internet Protection Act (CIPA) Rules
signals.summary: The FCC published revisions to the CIPA rules, including adding 'school board' to the definition of entities subject to CIPA certifications and incorporating statutory definitions of terms such as 'minor,' 'obscene,' 'child pornography,' and 'harmful to minors.' The revisions also clarify requirements for enforcing technology protection measures and mandate that Internet safety policies be made available to the Commission upon request.
key_provisions: Addition of 'school board' to CIPA certifications
Incorporation of statutory definitions for 'minor,' 'obscene,' 'child pornography,' and 'harmful to minors'
Clarification of requirements for technology protection measures
Mandate for Internet safety policies to be available to the FCC upon request

signals.title: FTC Announces Workshop on Age Verification Technologies
signals.summary: The FTC announced a virtual workshop scheduled for January 28, 2026, to examine age verification technologies. The workshop aimed to gather information on the effectiveness and challenges of current age verification methods and to explore potential improvements.
key_provisions: Gather information on effectiveness and challenges
